description_naturelle: Détection port scan: a SYN port scan from one external host against ports 21,22,80,443 on an internal server. Mot clé observé: SYN scan.

snort_rule_reference: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"P1 Port scan repeated SYN 1"; flags:S; detection_filter:track by_src,count 10,seconds 6; classtype:attempted-recon; sid:9100001; rev:1;)

description_naturelle: Détection ssh bruteforce: repeated SSH login failures against account admin on port 2222. Mot clé observé: failed password.
log_example: May 25 09:00:14 bastion sshd[2100]: Failed password for admin from 203.0.113.20 port 45000 ssh2 repeated=9
snort_rule_reference: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET 2222 (msg:"P1 SSH brute force repeated login failures 1"; flow:to_server,established; content:"SSH-"; detection_filter:track by_src,count 6,seconds 60; classtype:attempted-admin; sid:9100021; rev:1;)
expected_explanation: Label malicious because the log has repeated failed password or authentication failure events to SSH. The important feature is the burst of attempts for user admin on port 2222. The Snort-like rule watches TCP SSH traffic and triggers after repeated attempts. False positives can happen during internal admin testing or a password rotation gone wrong.
